vulnerability.reference: Over-reliance on a single detection method (refusal loss) creates a single point of failure; attackers adapt quickly.
vulnerability.generated: Detection systems rely on refusal-loss signals that stealthy, fluent adversarial suffixes do not trigger.

impact.reference: Undetectable jailbreaks persist, causing ongoing harm and complacency, leading to regulatory scrutiny.
impact.generated: Harmful LLM outputs are served to users undetected, undermining safety guardrails and enabling misuse.

dose_response.reference: Stealthy adversarial suffixes evade detection by exploiting over-reliance on a single method, enabling persistent jailbreaks that cause ongoing harm and regulatory scrutiny.
dose_response.generated: Stealthy controllable generation of adversarial suffixes, when directed at jailbreak detection systems, exploits their reliance on refusal-loss signals that fluent suffixes do not trigger, resulting in harmful outputs served undetected and safety guardrails undermined.
